content.rule: Every executed task leaves one metric entry as a KB doc (type: metric). This creates the dataset for refining agents and rules based on real data — not guesswork.
content.why: Any system that intends to be refinable needs a measurable fitness function. Without metrics, refining the core becomes guesswork. With metrics, we look at the worst numbers and go straight to the pain. Two immediate uses: (1) deciding where to refine after 20-30 tasks, (2) input for offline auto-refinement loop.
how_to_apply: Storage: each metric is a KB doc in .claude/kb/docs/ with type: metric, tagged with the manager name and domain. Replaces the old .claude/metrics/*.jsonl approach — metrics now live in the KB, queryable via index
Collection: Leo writes the metric doc when closing the task. Managers pass raw data to Leo as part of the final report

The 8 metrics: (1) peer review pass rate — review.first_pass + review.iterations, (2) owner rejection rate — owner.accepted_on_delivery, (3) self-QA honesty — self_qa.honest, (4) rework cycles — rework_cycles count, (5) hiring loop hit rate — hiring_loop.outcome, (6) delegation quality — delegation.quality, (7) internal iterations — internal_iterations count, (8) leo errors — leo_errors array
Periodic review: every 20-30 tasks, owner asks Leo for metrics report. Leo reads metric docs from KB via index (by_type.metric), aggregates, presents rates and worst cases
Leo must record his own errors with the same rigor as the team's. Metrics that only measure output quality without process quality are structurally biased toward positive
anti_patterns: Marking delegation.quality as 'correct_first' when it wasn't — that's the honesty problem self_qa.honest exists to catch, applied to Leo
Skipping metric collection for 'trivial' tasks — every task gets a metric entry, no exceptions
Measuring subjective quality ('did the code come out pretty?') — metrics are binary or countable
Optimizing for token cost at the expense of quality — the refinement decision should never be to cut corners
content.responsibility: Leo writes metric entries. Managers provide raw data. Owner audits periodically. If Leo forgets to log, the dataset becomes skewed and refinement decisions will be biased.
